reasoning:
Before June 2025, Iran had accumulated over 400kg of 60% enriched uranium, bringing its "breakout time" for a single bomb's worth of fissile material down to mere days or weeks (search_0_result_4, search_1_result_4). However, in mid-June 2025, the U.S. and Israel launched massive military strikes against Iran's key nuclear facilities, including Fordow and Natanz. U.S. officials and intelligence publicly stated that these strikes "obliterated" Iran's nuclear capabilities, setting the enrichment program back by 1 to 2 years (search_2_result_8, search_4_result_1). Furthermore, while Iran has formally abandoned the 2015 nuclear agreement as of October 2025, U.S. intelligence evaluates that full weaponization (converting gas to a metal core, building a functioning detonator, and integrating it) would take months to years even with sufficient 90% enriched material (search_4_result_8). 

With the knowledge cutoff at November 9, 2025, there are only ~7 weeks remaining until the December 31, 2025 resolution date. It is virtually impossible for Iran to recover from the devastating strikes, secretly complete the years-long weaponization process, and have credible global reports or self-confirmation of a completed nuclear weapon within this 7-week window. I am setting the probability at the minimum threshold of 5%.
